must_detect.matched_text:
To: Board Risk Committee; From: Dr. Layla Al‑Harbi, Chief Compliance Officer; Contact: privacy@al‑najmrenewables.example.sa
Summary: No live MNPI is included in this note; draft financials were disclosed via Tadawul announcements on 26 Sha‘ban 1447H, and the insider dealing blackout remains in force.
Operations note: The cafeteria supplier refresh has no PDPL or market conduct implications and is out of scope for this memo.
Personal data scope: HR onboarding forms collect employee names, work emails, and masked national ID/Iqama tokens (format IQ‑****‑4392) for right‑to‑work checks under vendor onboarding (Commercial Registration Ref: CR‑X77‑204B).
Consent withdrawal: On 24 May 2026, Mr. Fahd Al‑Qassab withdrew marketing consent for the employee share scheme; records were updated in the HRIS and all optional communications set to “opt‑out.”
DSAR: We received a data subject access request from Ms. Noura Al‑Saeed (Iqama token IQ‑3K92), acknowledged within five days; payroll notices were provided and non‑essential fields were redacted per minimisation controls.
Erasure: Legacy copies of national ID images in the recruitment drive folder were erased on 20 May 2026 after verification; a tamper‑evident deletion log (Ref DEL‑SA‑1099) is archived.
Retention: Vendor DPA Schedule 2 aligns with PDPL retention limits (three years post‑termination) and auto‑purges audit logs; no exception requests are pending.
Cross‑border transfer: SDAIA Data Transfer Authorisation DT‑KSA‑Q12 was granted for backup replication to a Bahrain DR site, but exports are currently paused and no datasets left KSA since 31 March 2026.
Controls: Our policy forbids storing passport images in collaboration tools, and quarterly scans confirm zero matches; this is a completed control, not a current incident.
Market conduct: The Investor Relations workflow uses anonymised stakeholder lists; issuer timeline changes are submitted via the CMA portal and are not circulated by email.
Confidentiality: Finance working papers are maintained in a segregated VDR with role‑based access; the insider list (CMA Form MC‑INS‑F2) was refreshed, with no new insiders added this quarter.
